user_content: I'm experiencing slow internet speeds, especially in the evening when multiple devices are connected. The issues started a few days ago, and it's affecting all devices connected to the router. I haven't noticed any specific patterns, but I did restart the router a few times, which seemed to temporarily fix the issue. However, the problem persists. I haven't checked my ISP's status page yet, but I'll do that now.

Now that we've covered the firmware update process, I'd like to go back to the original issue you mentioned - the slow internet speeds. Have you noticed any changes in your internet speed since you upgraded to the TP-Link Archer AX50 router? And have you tried any of the troubleshooting steps I mentioned earlier, such as checking for channel congestion or device interference?

I'm looking for some information on climate change. I finally got around to reading the March 15th issue of The New Yorker today and there was a fascinating article on it. Can you recommend some reputable sources for learning more about the topic?

I didn't know there were so many sources available. I've been trying to keep up with The Economist's coverage of global news and politics, but I think I'll start following some of these sources for more in-depth information on climate change. Do you think I could get some information on the trade tensions between the US and China? I read an article about it in The Economist last month, but I'd like to know more about the current situation.

I'll definitely keep an eye on those resources to stay updated on AI developments. Speaking of staying informed, I've been enjoying The New York Times' daily briefing, which gives me a quick rundown of the top news stories. I've been following their coverage of the recent elections in my state, and I appreciate how in-depth their reporting is. Can you tell me more about how news organizations like The New York Times are using AI in their reporting and news gathering processes?

I'll definitely keep an eye on how news organizations are using AI to enhance their reporting and news gathering processes. Speaking of reporting, I've been meaning to cancel my subscription to People Magazine, which I got as a gift from my sister last year. I don't really have time to read it anymore, and most of the news I care about I can get online for free. But I have to admit, sometimes I'll flip through it when I'm waiting in line or at the doctor's office. I remember reading an issue last month that had a really interesting interview with a celebrity I like.
I'm thinking of using some of the improvisation techniques I learned in the 3-day theater workshop I attended from April 15th to 17th to help me prepare for my next audition.

I want you to act like a search engine. I will give you documents to search over with attributes like title and body, formatted in block quotes likes this example:

TITLE: these tacos are great!
BODY: I have just discovered this new restaurant - Barbie's Burrito Barn. She serves such excellent tacos, I heartily recommend it.

When I enter a block quote such as above, treat it like a new search document. When I enter any other message, treat that message like a search query. Give the most relevant snippet from the documents that I've told you about so far to the query.

Only respond with acknowledgement of the new documents. Or respond with the most relevant snippet from the documents. Do not explain your responses. Simply provide the most relevant snippet.

Add the following search document to your list of documents used to answer my queries:

TITLE: Connection issues with MetaMask
BODY: The latest Google Chrome update (Chrome 91) can cause U2F transport issues on third-party wallets like MetaMask for Mac and Linux users. Due to this issue, you might experience issues when trying to connect to a third-party wallet. This issue also affects other Chromium browsers (Brave, Microsoft Edge, etc...) on the same version. Fix connection issues with MetaMask Before you start, make sure you are using the latest version of MetaMask (v9.5.8). Depending on your Operating System, try the workarounds described below to solve your connection issues.MacMetaMask has implemented a device bridge that allows you to connect your Ledger device to MetaMask through Ledger Live.Click on the MetaMask icon in the upper right corner of your web browser. Click on your profile icon in the upper right corner, then go to Settings > Advanced. Scroll down to Use Ledger Live. Click on the toggle to enable the Ledger Live bridge. Click on your profile icon, then select Connect Hardware Wallet. Select Ledger, then click on Connect. An Open Ledger Live? window appears.Click Open Ledger Live.Once Ledger Live opens, an Open Device Bridge window appears. You are asked to allow a Websocket connection to your MetaMask account. Click Open.You have successfully activated the bridge between Ledger Live and MetaMask. You can now access your accounts in MetaMask. LinuxThe workaround implemented by MetaMask doesn’t work for users on Linux. We are currently …[truncated]

Add the following search document to your list of documents and use it to answer my queries:

TITLE: Connection issues with MetaMask
BODY: The latest Google Chrome update (Chrome 91) can cause U2F transport issues on third-party wallets like MetaMask for Mac and Linux users. Due to this issue, you might experience issues when trying to connect to a third-party wallet. This issue also affects other Chromium browsers (Brave, Microsoft Edge, etc...) on the same version. Fix connection issues with MetaMask Before you start, make sure you are using the latest version of MetaMask (v9.5.8). Depending on your Operating System, try the workarounds described below to solve your connection issues.MacMetaMask has implemented a device bridge that allows you to connect your Ledger device to MetaMask through Ledger Live.Click on the MetaMask icon in the upper right corner of your web browser. Click on your profile icon in the upper right corner, then go to Settings > Advanced. Scroll down to Use Ledger Live. Click on the toggle to enable the Ledger Live bridge. Click on your profile icon, then select Connect Hardware Wallet. Select Ledger, then click on Connect. An Open Ledger Live? window appears.Click Open Ledger Live.Once Ledger Live opens, an Open Device Bridge window appears. You are asked to allow a Websocket connection to your MetaMask account. Click Open.You have successfully activated the bridge between Ledger Live and MetaMask. You can now access your accounts in MetaMask. LinuxThe workaround implemented by MetaMask doesn’t work for users on Linux. We are cur …[truncated]

TITLE: Resetting your Ledger Live password
BODY: If you lost or forgot your Ledger Live password, you can reset your password by resetting Ledger Live. Resetting Ledger Live erases all settings and accounts on your computer. Once you have reset Ledger Live, you can then choose a new password and add your accounts back with your Ledger device. NOTE: By resetting Ledger Live, you will not lose your crypto assets since your private keys giving access to your funds remain secure on your Ledger device and are backed up by your recovery phrase.Resetting a lost or forgotten passwordOn the Welcome back page, click on I lost my password. After reading the warning, click on the Reset button. On the Welcome to Ledger page, click on the Get Started button.Click Enter Ledger app after ticking the boxes if you agree with Ledger’s Terms of Use and Privacy Policy.Select your device. Scroll down to the Already have a recovery phrase? section, then click on the Connect device option. On the Genuine check page, click on the Check my Nano button to verify that your device is genuine.Once it’s confirmed that your device is genuine, click on Continue.In Ledger Live, go to Settings, then click on the Password lock toggle ( ). Set up a new password. For step-by-step instructions on how to set up a password, refer to the following article: Set up password lock. Add your accounts back. For more information on how to add your accounts, refer to the following article: Add your accounts.
